texto: I wasn’t about to tell him that I never said anything to anyone who teased me. I just went along with it like it was my joke too. I wanted everyone to like me…
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sydney Salter: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I wasn’t about to tell him that I never said anything to anyone who teased me. I just went along …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, fear, about pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: The gods wanted to create creatures who would worship them. First they made the animals, but they couldn’t talk. Then the gods created people out of mud, but when it rained, they fell apart. Then the gods created people out of wood, but they had no feelings, so the gods washed them away in a big flood. Finally, the gods created people out of corn, and they turned out just right.

frase_original: The gods wanted to create creatures who would worship them. First they made the animals, but they couldn’t talk. Then the gods created people out of mud, but when it rained, they fell apart. Then the gods created people out of wood, but they had no feelings, so the gods washed them away in a big flood. Finally, the gods created people out of corn, and they turned out just right.
